text:
<think>I want to start with a general statement about language evolution to set the stage.</think>
Languages evolve as speakers change the way they use words, grammar, and pronunciation over time.  
<think>Now I should explain what causes these changes, to add context to the process.</think>
These changes can be influenced by contact with other languages, technological advances, social shifts, and the natural tendency of language to simplify or innovate.  
<think>Next, I want to transition into language death and why some languages disappear, connecting it to evolution.</think>
Some languages die out when their speakers shift to using another, often more dominant, language for economic, political, or cultural reasons.  
<think>I should clarify what happens as a result of this shift and why it leads to language death.</think>
When children stop learning a language as their first language, it gradually loses its speakers and can eventually become extinct.  
<think>To finish, I should summarize and tie both concepts together.</think>
In summary, languages constantly evolve, but some disappear entirely when social and historical pressures favor other languages instead.
